digest.component_or_area: test-coverage remediation epic (compaction, durability, CLI validation, docs)
propositions: The compaction rollback branch in compact.py unconditionally removes the snapshot; it must retain the snapshot on incomplete rollback, verified by a fault-injection test.
The compaction fold must be provably all-or-none under forward- and reverse-rename failure and recoverable after real process death.
Whitespace-only ticket titles are wrongly accepted and must be rejected across CLI, library, and MCP create/edit paths.
Async-push, remote-append, and parent-cascade contracts need tests exercising real process/clone-level behavior rather than in-process simulations.
Stale docs must be corrected: concurrency.md detached-HEAD claim, README flock framing (actually fcntl+mkdir), and mutation-testing.md score scope.
The report's HIGH-priority concurrency-storm xfail self-disable claim is invalid and excluded from scheduled work.
